data.title: Digital Realty Trust enters Malaysia through CSF Advisers acquisition
data.description:
Digital Realty Trust (DLR) on Monday announced an entry into Malaysia through the acquisition of CSF Advisers, a Greater Kuala Lumpur-based data center hub.

The expansion into Malaysia builds on the data center REIT's established Southeast Asia presence in Singapore and Jakarta.

CSF Advisers-owned TelcoHub 1 is an operational 1.5 megawatt data center.

In conjunction with TelcoHub 1, Digital Realty has also agreed to acquire adjacent land that can support up to 14 megawatts of IT load.

The acquisitions are expected to close in the first half of 2026.

data.title: Wedgewood Partners adds AMZN and CB; exits POOL
data.description:
• Wedgewood Partners Fund  underperformed with a loss of 1.8% in Q4 2025, compared to a gain of 1.1% and 2.7% for the Russell 1000 Growth Index and S&P 500, respectively, for the same period.
  • During Q4 2025, the fund established new positions in Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Chubb (NYSE:CB).
  • The fund sold its investment in Pool (NASDAQ:POOL).
  • Source.

data.title: ClearBridge International Growth EAFE Strategy exits Novo Nordisk, adds Roche in Q4
data.description:
• The ClearBridge International Growth EAFE Strategy underperformed its MSCI EAFE Index benchmark and MSCI EAFE Growth Index secondary benchmark during the fourth quarter of 2025.
  • The Strategy produced positive contributions across five of the nine sectors in which it was invested.
  • Financials and IT sectors were the top contributors, while consumer discretionary sector was the chief detractor.
  • "In particular, stock selection in consumer discretionary, health care, industrials, materials, and financials and an underweight to financials weighed on results," said the Q4 fund letter.
  • "On the positive side, stock selection in IT and an underweight to communication services contributed to performance," added the fund letter.
  • The fund exited Novo Nordisk (NVO) (NONOF) (NVON:CA), Linde (LIN), and UniCredit (UNCFF) (UNCRY), and sold its stake in Trane Technologies (TT) and Nemetschek (NEMTF) in the fourth quarter.
  • ClearBridge International Growth EAFE Strategy closed its position in Constellation Software (CNSWF) (CNSWY) (CSU:CA), Lenovo (LNVGY) (LNVGF), Marks and Spencer (MAKSF) (MAKSY), Tokio Marine (TKOMY) (TKOMF), and Sony Financial (SFGYY).
  • Ryanair Holdings (RYAAY) (RYAOF) was repurchased, while Heidelberg Materials (HDLMY) (HLBZF) was added during the quarter.
  • Additionally, the fund repurchased EssilorLuxottica (ESLOF) (ESLOY) and Galderma Group (GALDY) (GDERF) and added Roche (RHHBY) (RHHBF) (RHHVF).
  • Source: Fund Letter

data.title: Rewey Asset Management adds DFIN
data.description:
• The RAM Smid Composite returned 3.58% in Q4 2025, outperforming its benchmark, the Russell 2500 Value Total Return Index, which rose 3.15%.
  • The fund initiated a new position in Donnelley Financial Solutions (NYSE:DFIN) during Q4 2025.
  • Source.

data.title: Oakmark Fund adds AER, AMRZ, and CDW; exits BK and MGA
data.description:
• Oakmark Fund (Investor Class) returned 4.78%, outperforming the S&P 500 Index’s 2.66%.
  • During Q4 2025, the fund added new positions in AerCap (NYSE:AER), Amrize (NYSE:AMRZ), and CDW (NASDAQ:CDW) to the portfolio.
  • In Q4 2025, the fund exited its positions in Bank of New York Mellon (BK) and Magna (MGA).
  • Source.

data.title: Oakmark Global Select Fund adds SNY; exits MBGAF and RHHBY
data.description:
• Oakmark Global Select Fund (Investor Class) underperformed the MSCI World Index for Q4 2025, the full year, and since inception.
  • During Q4 2025, the fund added new positions in Sanofi (SNY) to the portfolio.
  • In Q4 2025, the fund exited its positions in Mercedes-Benz Group (MBGAF) and Roche Holding (RHHBY).
  • Source.

data.title: Oakmark Equity And Income Fund exits BlackRock, adds Mondelez in Q4
data.description:
• Oakmark Equity and Income Fund outperformed the benchmark S&P 500 and Bloomberg U.S. Aggregate Bond Index during the fourth quarter.
  • The largest contributors to the performance were communication services and health care, while industrials were detractors.
  • The quarterly performance was marked by a market rally, with many value and smaller capitalization stocks performing well.
  • The fund initiated a new position in Amrize (AMRZ), Gartner (IT), and Mondelez International (MDLZ).
  • The fund exited BlackRock (BLK) and Charter Communications (CHTR).
  • Source: Fund Letter

data.title: SA Asks: What are the most attractive REIT stocks right now?
data.description:
What are the most attractive REIT stocks for investors right now?

Seeking Alpha analysts Ivo Kolchev and IWA Research weigh in.

Ivo Kolchev: When it comes to REITs, I advise readers to diversify across cyclical sectors (such as hotels and retail) and defensive sectors (such as healthcare and residential). Obviously, the exact mix will depend on individual investor circumstances, but being prepared for downside surprises in economic growth is pertinent. I also expect REIT M&A to pick up in 2027; hence, I would give a preference to smaller REITs that have a chance of being acquired.

For U.S. markets, hotel REITs such as RLJ Lodging Trust (RLJ) look particularly attractive in light of an expected rebound in U.S. GDP growth and a packed 2026 event calendar, most notably the FIFA World Cup. Some other cyclical REITs to consider would be CBL & Associates Properties (CBL) and Orion Properties (ONL), which offer attractive valuations but also carry a lot of debt, which is a risk to consider. Defensive REIT picks would include healthcare REITs such as Community Healthcare Trust (CHCT) and Medical Properties Trust (MPW).

For Europe, I would highlight German residential players such as Vonovia (VONOY) and LEG Immobilien (LEGIF), which offer mid-single-digit dividends and ample capital gain potential as they trade well below book value, which is expected to rise in 2026. Some smaller cyclical names would include retail REITs such as Wereldhave Belgium (WRDEF) and Immobiliare Grande Distribuzione (IMMQF), which trade at cap rates of about 8% to 9% despite solid occupancy of about 95%.

IWA Research: My pick would be RLJ Lodging Trust (RLJ), a REIT that owns premium-branded hotels across the U.S., currently paying a nearly 8% dividend yield. Despite the stock's weakness in recent years that I believe is generated by industry-wide macro pressure, the company stands to benefit significantly in 2026 from unique factors such as the World Cup and America 250, while still trading at significantly undervalued levels. They are also in a very strong financial position for a REIT, with significant cash on hand, manageable leverage, and a PB of around 0.52, while still generating a solid AFFO.

data.title: PennantPark Investment estimates NII per share at $0.13-$0.15 for FQ1
data.description:
PennantPark Investment (PNNT) estimated its core net investment income for the fiscal first quarter ended December 31, 2025, between $0.13 and $0.15 per share, vs. $0.15 in the prior quarter.

Net investment income is estimated to be between $0.10 and $0.12 per share, compared to $0.15 per share in the prior quarter.

Net asset value per share is estimated to be between $6.97 and $7.02 per share at December 31, 2025, compared to $7.11 at September 30, 2025.

data.title: LVMH slides in Paris trading after Morgan Stanley pulls its bull rating
data.description:
Morgan Stanley downgraded LVMH Moët Hennessy - Louis Vuitton (LVMHF) (LVMUY) to an Equal-weight rating on Monday after having the stock set at Overweight.

Analyst Edouard Aubin and his team see greater downside than upside risk to EPS consensus numbers for 2026 due largely to FX and tariff headwinds, as well as other issues such as the wines & spirits division's top line likely remaining under pressure.

Aubin warned that LVMH (LVMHF) (LVMUY) will be only partially able to offset the FX and tariff headwinds through price increases given the necessity to not price out the middle-income consumer. As for the wine & spirits business, Aubin noted that industry data points to a number of challenges intensifying, such as changing consumer behaviors, competition from other alcohol players, and increased tariffs in the key U.S. and Chinese markets. "We now model that the W&S division's profit will fall to €988m (18.1% margin) in 2026, the lowest level since 2010," wrote Aubin.

The overall takeaway is that the stock is unlikely to continue to appreciate in the coming weeks.

Morgan Stanley set a price target of €635 on LVMH (LVMHF).

LVMH (LVMHF) (LVMUY) operates six main business lines that don't always see the same peaks and valleys in terms of demand and sales. The Paris-based company's business lines are wines and spirits, fashion and leather goods, perfumes and cosmetics, watches and jewelry, selective retailing (notably Sephora and travel retail), and other activities including hospitality and media.

Shares of LVMH (LVMHF) (LVMUY) fell 4.1% in Paris trading after the ratings slash from Morgan Stanley.
